capture.label: Capture Enabled
capture.description: Start or stop keyboard event capture

ipc.title: IPC Commands
ipc.toggleCommand: Toggle Capture: qs -c noctalia-shell ipc call plugin:show-keys toggle

security.title: Security Disclaimer
security.message: Show Keys reads raw keyboard events from /dev/input/event*. Compositors such as niri do not currently expose a native secure API for this use case, so this plugin must rely on direct input-device access. Granting your user access to the 'input' group weakens Wayland's input isolation and may allow other processes running as your user to observe key presses.
security.disclaimer: Until niri or other Wayland compositors provide an appropriate native API, this plugin operates with that security tradeoff. If you enable it, you accept and assume the associated risk.

device.label: Device Path (IMPORTANT)
device.description: Your keyboard device event path, RESTART noctalia-shell to apply changes.

guide.title: Setup Guide
guide.steps:
• FIRST-TIME users: RUN 'sudo evtest' to find your keyboard device path.
• For non-root capture, ADD your user to the 'input' group with 'sudo usermod -aG input $USER', then REBOOT or log in again.
• WARNING: this is a convenience tradeoff and may allow other processes running as your user to read raw keyboard input.
• If you do not accept that risk, do not enable this plugin until compositor-native support exists.

position.label: OSD Position
position.description: Where the key overlay appears on screen

delay.label: Auto-hide Delay
delay.description: Seconds before OSD fades out: {value} s

pillColor.label: Pill Text Color
pillColor.description: Color of the key label text

pillBg.label: Pill Background Color
pillBg.description: Background color of each key pill

useCustomColors.label: Use Custom Colors
useCustomColors.description: Override your Noctalia shell theme colors

monitors.label: Active Monitors
monitors.description: Select which monitors will display the key overlay
